title: Oxylobium ilicifolium
taxonomy: Oxylobium ilicifolium is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, which is commonly known as the legume family. It is native to Australia and is also found in New Zealand. The plant belongs to the subfamily Faboideae, which contains about 20,000 species. Oxylobium ilicifolium has been assigned to the tribe Podalyrieae, a group of plants that are mostly found in southern Africa and Australia. intro: Oxylobium ilicifolium, also known as the Holly-leaved Oxylobium, is a flowering plant native to Australia, characterized by its distinctive holly-like leaves. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Oxylobium ilicifolium include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in topical applications to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
